response.justification: The candidate brings 17+ years of broad software engineering experience and genuine entrepreneurial initiative, but falls significantly short of the principal/director-level AI strategy profile this role demands. AI-related work appears only in the most recent role (April 2025) and is limited to MCP tooling infrastructure rather than demonstrated AI product leadership, strategic advisory, or ML system design. There is no evidence of cloud architecture expertise at scale, no executive or customer-advisory experience, and no publications, recognized AI contributions, or products built with AI at a level consistent with a Principal Engineer advising strategic enterprise customers. The career arc is that of a strong individual contributor generalist, not a technical strategy leader.
strengths: 17+ years of continuous hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, satisfying the minimum experience threshold
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective, shipped multiple open-source libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Vibe.dav), and delivered a real product used by Green Map globally
Recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq signals awareness of the AI tooling space and willingness to move toward the domain
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent (April 2025 onward); no demonstrated history building AI-powered products, training models, or leading AI platform strategy at any scale

No evidence of strategic advisory, executive partnering, or customer-facing technical leadership — the role requires advising enterprise partners and driving organizational AI adoption, not IC delivery

Cloud expertise is incidental (AWS CDK for pricing workflows at Optilyz); no cloud architecture leadership, platform ownership, or hyperscaler-level experience consistent with advising on cloud AI strategy
